top.title: FBT003: False positive with positional-only parameters
top.why_pick_this: Maintainer explicitly said 'I would accept that patch' and pointed to the exact file. Clear acceptance criteria, bounded scope, and Ruff is a high-recognition repo. Rust + static analysis is substantive resume material with a crisp before/after narrative.
top.first_step: Clone ruff, open crates/ruff_linter/src/rules/flake8_boolean_trap/helpers.rs, and write a failing test fixture using a function with a `*args` parameter and another with positional-only `/` syntax to lock in the bug before touching logic.

top.title: Handling of loggers with propagate=False
top.why_pick_this: A maintainer already has PR #14375 in flight and is soliciting feedback/testing — lowest-risk merge path on the list. pytest is universally recognized, and 'fixed log capture for non-propagating loggers' is a clean one-sentence interview story about Python logging internals.
top.first_step: Check out PR #14375 locally, run its tests against a minimal repro (logger with propagate=False emitting during a test), and post a substantive review comment or follow-up commit addressing any gaps — co-authorship is a faster path here than a competing PR.

top.title: OpenAPI discriminator field disappeared with pydantic v2.5
top.why_pick_this: Confirmed regression with maintainer acknowledgment, bisectable between two known versions, and high real-world impact (OpenAPI consumers). Regression-fix narratives interview extremely well: 'I bisected, found the commit, wrote a test, fixed it.' Pydantic is a top-tier repo for resume value.
top.first_step: Write a pytest case using a discriminated union model and assert the `discriminator` key is present in `model_json_schema()` output; confirm it passes on v2.3 and fails on v2.5, then `git bisect` to find the offending commit.

top.title: Using monkeypatch in non function scoped fixture
top.why_pick_this: Maintainer explicitly approved the design in 2019 with `tmp_path_factory` as the pattern to follow — no architectural risk. Scope is well-bounded (mirror an existing fixture), and 'added session-scoped monkeypatch to pytest core' is an instantly understandable contribution on a resume.
top.first_step: Read src/_pytest/monkeypatch.py and src/_pytest/tmpdir.py side by side to map the factory-fixture pattern, then sketch the `monkeypatch_session` fixture signature and finalization in a draft commit before opening a discussion PR.

top.title: Tracking issue for missing methods in `arr` namespace
top.why_pick_this: Bounded scope (pick one method), clear precedent PRs to mirror (#23150, #24472), and active maintainer guidance. Polars is a hot repo and Rust+pyo3 work is strong technical signal. Risk is low because each method is a small, pattern-following PR — pick `arr.mean` first as the simplest.
top.first_step: Read PR #23150 (arr.slice/head/tail) end-to-end to internalize the pattern, then scaffold `arr.mean` by copying the corresponding `list.mean` implementation in crates/polars-ops/src/chunked_array/array/ and wiring up the Python binding.
